title: Click event storage for audit/analytics
description: Add a listing_clicks table and record one row per redirect: listing_id, clicked_at, source, context (which surface the click came from), and a nullable user_id FK reserved for Phase 3 attribution.
acceptance_criteria: New listing_clicks table added via the existing additive _MIGRATIONS pattern in db.py (idempotent, no separate migration tool introduced)
Every successful /out/<listing_id> redirect writes exactly one listing_clicks row before issuing the 302
listing_clicks.user_id is nullable and always NULL until Phase 3 (EPIC-103) begins writing it
listing_clicks.context records which page/surface the click originated from (dashboard/project/auctions at minimum)
No PII beyond an optional hashed IP is stored; raw IP addresses are not persisted
A failed click-record write does not prevent the redirect from completing (never block the user's navigation on analytics)
notes: See docs/adr/0002-affiliate-link-redirect-and-tracking.md. Table shape anticipates Phase 3 (EPIC-103) attribution without requiring a second migration. Shipped 2026-07-08: the acceptance criteria's '_MIGRATIONS pattern' was imprecise — _MIGRATIONS is ALTER-TABLE-ADD-COLUMN-only in db.py; a brand new table follows the existing CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pattern in _SCHEMA instead (same as auction_snapshots/source_runs), which is what shipped. context covers dashboard/project/auctions/offers/duplicate_review/unknown — offers and duplicate_review added beyond the minimum named here per the broader gateway brief. Hashed-IP storage was evaluated and deliberately not implemented (no abuse/fraud need identified yet for a single-operator tool; adding a column nobody populates contradicts 'minimum schema needed') — see implementation notes for the full reasoning: docs/implementation-notes/2026-07-08-2135-marketplace-outbound-gateway-epic-101.md.
